source.name: Compeller by Niall Keegan
source.author: Niall Keegan
source.description:
Want to play an Enchanter in 2024 5e? Don't want to wait for WotC to release one officially? Step right this way. And then left this way. Now hop up and down. This is fun. You are having fun...
Features of the Compeller Wizard include:
• Enchantment Savant: As well as everything you'd normally expect from this feature, you can also use it to learn Wisdom-save Wizard spells from other schools. • Dazzle: Take a Bonus Action to impose a -1d4 penalty on your target's next save, or -1d6 if it's a save against an Enchantment spell. • Slip of the Mind: When your Concentration spells end, take a Reaction to force a Wisdom save from all Charmed targets. If they fail, they forget you cast the spell! • Mental Barricade: Add your INT mod to your Wisdom saving throws. • Compelling Weave: Don't you hate it when you land an important spell, only for the target to make an INT/WIS/CHA save at the end of their turn and wriggle out of it? Well, now they always have disadvantage on those rolls. • All-Charmer: What's this "Immunity" nonsense? Creatures with Charmed Immunity - sorry, creatures that think they have Charmed Immunity actually only have advantage on saves against being Charmed by your spells.
